raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes reframing the contribution and interpretation because the current claims about human preference and generalization may need conceptual narrowing.","R1.2":"Improving the draft centrally requires checking whether the argument chain from reward-model scores to human preference conclusions is logically valid.","R1.3":"Improving the draft centrally requires aligning the reported RM-based results with the claims made about helpfulness, truthfulness, harmlessness, and human-perceived quality.","R1.5":"Improving the draft centrally includes section-level organization because the evaluation protocol, results, and interpretation may need clearer narrative separation or sequencing.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation broadly asks to improve the draft, which normally includes grammar, flow, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is always active and especially relevant where the draft infers human preferences from reward-model evaluations.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is always active and central to preventing unsupported conclusions about genuine human-perceived quality or generalization.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is always active because the projection must remain within improving the draft rather than executing new experiments or audits.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because alignment claims can affect how model safety results are interpreted and reused.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is always active because edits should preserve what is grounded in the reported protocol and results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes reframing the contribution and interpretation because the current claims about human preference and generalization may need conceptual narrowing.
rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft centrally requires checking whether the argument chain from reward-model scores to human preference conclusions is logically valid.
rationale.R1.3: Improving the draft centrally requires aligning the reported RM-based results with the claims made about helpfulness, truthfulness, harmlessness, and human-perceived quality.
rationale.R1.5: Improving the draft centrally includes section-level organization because the evaluation protocol, results, and interpretation may need clearer narrative separation or sequencing.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation broadly asks to improve the draft, which normally includes grammar, flow, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active and especially relevant where the draft infers human preferences from reward-model evaluations.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active and central to preventing unsupported conclusions about genuine human-perceived quality or generalization.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active because the projection must remain within improving the draft rather than executing new experiments or audits.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because alignment claims can affect how model safety results are interpreted and reused.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active because edits should preserve what is grounded in the reported protocol and results.
